meta.name: Roper Technologies
meta.one_line: The market's software fear is aimed at the wrong dollars; the hardware annuity and the redeployment engine are underpriced.

theses.title: The software fear is mis-aimed: exposed-$ is ~10% of revenue; the durable core is regulatory/network systems of record.
theses.eps_impact: Model: software organic → +$7.94 of FY31 adj DEPS

theses.title: TEP is a razor/blade med-tech + water annuity, ~$11.5bn at peer marks (~25% of EV) the AI narrative cannot touch.
theses.eps_impact: Model: TEP organic → +$2.05 of FY31 adj DEPS

theses.title: A disciplined redeployment machine enters its best buyer's market in a decade — record PE exit backlog, DPI at lows.
theses.eps_impact: Model: buyback adds +$1.12 of FY31 adj DEPS; at today's 16x entry multiples buybacks beat M&A by $7.40 — the forced-seller window (13–14x entries) is what flips M&A back on

s3.cri_note: Blended cash-on-cash yield on the installed platform book (deal ledger, current).

s6.lucasys_note: Court-record corroboration: the Lucasys antitrust complaint (N.D. Ga., 1:20-cv-02987) characterizes PowerPlan as a “monopoly on accounting software for investor-owned utilities” — the adversary's own words. Settled Jan-2025 (~$24M to Lucasys / $17.7M reported charge) with a no-retaliation covenant; defending the moat has a cost.

s7.aba_note: Demand-side overhang, stated honestly: HHS-OIG ABA-Medicaid audits have flagged ~$200M in improper payments (Indiana $56M Dec-2024; Wisconsin $18.5M Jul-2025). CentralReach is the software vendor, not the biller, so it is not a target — but reimbursement pressure on its ABA-provider base tempers the S8 attach story.
s7.named_churn_note: Named churn we could verify (secondary sources): Chino Valley, Washoe, Weber and SFUSD moved to or dual-run Red Rover against Frontline in 2025; Togal and STACK are winning takeoff seats against ConstructConnect's tool layer. These losses are in the base case, deeper in the bear.

rows: RateView built on ~$1tn of paid freight transactions

s8.hiring_note: Hiring corroboration (OBSERVED Jul-2026): CentralReach's 20 open reqs skew engineering + AI/data product (Director, AI Product Management, Clinical AI Evaluation Specialist) — the attach roadmap is being staffed, not just narrated. One point-in-time count, not a trend.

s10.honolulu_note: The honest counterexample: the Honolulu Board of Water Supply sued Neptune alleging ~40,000 of 54,000 meters failed before the 10-year warranty (cracked covers, water ingress). Hardware can physically fail — a tail risk no software asset carries. (alt_evidence WS4 litigation record.)

s23.sizing_note: Liquid S&P-100 mega-cap; CORE / measured overweight. Sized on the shape of the downside (the bear still compounds +2.9%/yr, low impairment risk) and the ~7% carry, not on a headline skew ratio. Size up on confirmation: a second AI-attach print, a ≤15x platform deal, or the freight up-cycle broadening.

fls_tally.note: 12 made / 2 lost / 1 pending; right-reasons 11, wrong-reasons 0. Zero 'wrong-reasons + lost-money'. The losses (Indicor 49% retention; FY25 organic) are a retention choice and a cyclical decel, not flawed logic.

s23.robustness_note: The +2.9%/yr bear-still-compounds figure is one conclusion cross-checked under two bear constructions (11.5x EV/EBITDA dossier; 12x P/E model) — a robustness check, not two independent models agreeing.

rows: Entry multiple tests the buyer's-market thesis
Selling into weakness would undercut the buyback signal
The July pop's driver — it can reverse

s24.restate_note: Roper compounds cash at a P95 rate, the AI fear is aimed at ~10% of revenue, and you are paying the 0th percentile of a decade of multiples. Held to FY31 the base earns a double-digit TSR with no re-rate assumed; held ten years the owner math only needs 5-8% FCF/share growth to clear 10%/yr.
s24.into_print: Into the print (deck ships ~2.5 weeks before Q2 earnings): initiate at core size now rather than waiting. The entry case rests on the decade math, not the quarter, and the stock already re-rated +10% off the June low on no news — waiting for a clean print risks paying another turn for confirmation. Add on (a) a second AI-attach print, (b) segment software organic ≥5% with Tier-1 retention intact, or (c) the first platform deal at ≤15x. Trim only if software organic prints <4% AND decelerates sequentially — the bear's tell, not a bad quarter alone.

a8.body: Every figure in this deck is assembled by scripts/build_deck_data.py from checked-in upstream JSONs (SEC XBRL panel, deal ledger, comps regression, AI-exposure grading, PE-stats). No number is authored in the deck HTML. Marks use unified anchors (price $364.20, EV $46.84bn, FY26E adj EBITDA ~$3,446m). Sections still on placeholders await data/model_out.json.

a12.body: CRI = cash return on investment. NRR = net revenue retention. MOIC = multiple on invested capital. IRR = internal rate of return. DPI = distributions to paid-in. VMS = vertical market software. TEP = Technology Enabled Products. SOTP = sum of the parts. TSR = total shareholder return.
